title: mentha arvensis var. palustris
intro: Mentha arvensis var. palustris, also known as marsh pennyroyal or pale pennyroyal, is a variant of the wild mint plant native to Europe, Asia, and North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis var. palustris include its ability to relieve nausea, improve digestion, and reduce fever, as well as its potential to aid in the management of menstrual cramps and other women's health issues. This plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and oils, and is often used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy for its calming and invigorating effects.
